pattern: Dijkstra from node 0 with the extra constraint that reaching node i is only valid if the arrival time is strictly less than disappear[i].
hints: Run Dijkstra starting from node 0; initialize dist[0] = 0 (node 0 is reachable at time 0 because 0 < disappear[0]).
When relaxing an edge to neighbor v with cost len, only update if the new arrival time < disappear[v].
After Dijkstra completes, set any node whose dist is still infinity (or was never reached) to -1 in the output.

edge_inputs.description: node 1 disappears exactly at arrival time — unreachable
node 1 disappears one step after arrival — reachable
indirect path is faster than direct
node 0 has small disappear but we start there at time 0

stress.description: random sparse graph with disappear times
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = size
    edges = []
    for _ in range(size):
        u = rng.randint(0, n-1)
        v = rng.randint(0, n-1)
        if u != v:
            l = rng.randint(1, 100)
            edges.append([u, v, l])
    disappear = [rng.randint(1, 1000) for _ in range(n)]
    disappear[0] = max(disappear[0], 1)  # node 0 must be accessible at time 0
    return [n, edges, disappear]
